---
title: "Project Layout"
description: "Complete reference for the directory structure of a Composez project."
---

## Full Layout

A complete Composez project looks like this:

```
my-novel/
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .composez                    # Project configuration (levels, etc.)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .vale.ini                    # Vale linter config (created on first lint)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .vale-styles/                # Downloaded Vale style packages
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .git/                        # Git repository
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .env                         # API keys (optional)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .aider.conf.yml              # Aider settings (optional)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .gitignore
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CLAUDE.md                    # Auto-generated project guide for AI tools
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ novel/                       # All narrative content
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Act 1 - The Beginning/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ outline.md           # Any .md file (user notes)
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Chapter 1 - Arrivals/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ notes.md         # Any .md file (user notes)
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Scene 1 - The Train Station/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SUMMARY.md   # Scene synopsis
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ PROSE.md     # Scene prose
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Scene 2 - First Impressions/
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SUMMARY.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ PROSE.md
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Chapter 2 - Settling In/
в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Act 2 - Rising Action/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ ...
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ db/                          # Reference database (read-only context)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ core/                    # Always-loaded context
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ metadata.yml         # Book title and author
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ style.md             # Style guide
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ characters/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ elena.md
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ tom.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ locations/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ lighthouse.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ items/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ themes/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ style/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ notes/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ snippets/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ other/
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ instructions/                # Reusable instruction files
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tone-shift.md
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ action-scenes.md
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ cache/                       # Save/load slots (auto-created)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ chat/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ default.yml
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ context/
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ default.yml
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ cover.jpg                    # Cover image (optional, used by EPUB export)
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ export.md                    # Export output (created by /export)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ export.docx
в””в”Ђв”Ђ export.epub
```

## Key Directories

### `novel/`

All narrative content. The constant `NOVEL_DIR = "novel"` is hardcoded in `composez_core/config.py`.

**Structure rules:**

- Directories follow the pattern `Level N - Title` (e.g., `Act 1 - The Beginning`)
- Non-leaf levels (Act, Chapter) can contain any `.md` files
- Leaf levels (Scene) can only contain `SUMMARY.md` and `PROSE.md`
- The `edit_path_validator` hook enforces these rules at the coder level

### `db/`

Reference material organized by category. Each category is a subdirectory.

**Special behavior:**

- `db/core/` files are **always loaded** as read-only context
- Other categories are loaded on demand via `/add db`
- The AI is instructed not to edit db files during normal chat

### `instructions/`

Reusable directive files loaded via `/instruct`. Can be `.md` or `.txt`.

### `cache/`

Auto-created by `/save`. Contains YAML serializations of chat history (`cache/chat/`) and file context (`cache/context/`).

## Important Files

### `.composez`

YAML configuration. See [.composez Configuration](composez-config.qmd).

### `db/core/metadata.yml`

Book metadata used by EPUB export:

```yaml
title: The Lighthouse
author: Elena Vasquez
```

### `db/core/style.md`

Style guide loaded as context for every AI interaction. The most impactful file for prose quality.

### `SUMMARY.md`

Scene synopsis. Exists only at leaf level. Used as context when writing adjacent scenes.

### `PROSE.md`

Scene prose. Exists only at leaf level. This is the text that appears in your exported manuscript.

### `.vale.ini`

Vale linter configuration. Created automatically on first `/lint` with fiction-friendly defaults. See [Prose Linting](../guide/prose-linting.qmd).

### `CLAUDE.md`

Auto-generated on project initialization. Describes the project structure so other AI tools (Claude Code, etc.) can understand and work with the project correctly.

### `cover.jpg` / `cover.png`

Optional cover image used by EPUB export. A placeholder SVG is created on project initialization.

## Naming Conventions

### Directory Names

```
Level N - Title
```

- **Level**: matches a configured level name (Act, Chapter, Scene, etc.)
- **N**: ordinal number (1-based, auto-incremented by `/new`)
- **Title**: optional, human-readable description

Examples:

```
Act 1 - The Awakening
Chapter 3 - Revelations
Scene 12 - The Duel
```

### Database Entry Names

Lowercase, no spaces preferred. The filename (minus extension) is the entry name:

```
db/characters/elena.md    в†’ entry name: elena
db/locations/lighthouse.md в†’ entry name: lighthouse
```

### Instruction Names

The filename stem is the instruction name:

```
instructions/tone-shift.md  в†’ /instruct tone-shift
instructions/pacing.txt     в†’ /instruct pacing
```
